why_now: Gemma 3 NVFP4-KV corruption is localized below the Python-visible FlashInfer wrapper; the generated-module/C++ identity and tensor logging now proves the live paged-prefill binding is not specialized as FP4 KV.
acceptance: Run the packet with jethac/flashinfer@96be2fa8 and FLASHINFER_PREFILL_DEBUG_ONCE=1.
Capture generated-module/C++ identity and bound tensor evidence for the failing paged-prefill wrapper.
Run scripts/flashinfer_prefill_debug_log_audit.py and preserve its JSON result.
Preserve server log and summary artifact even if output remains corrupt.
finding: Both SWA and global FlashInfer paged-prefill calls compile as dtype_kv=uint8_t with require_fp4_kv=0, is_kv_fp4x2=0, empty additional_tensors, and no maybe_k_cache_sf/maybe_v_cache_sf tensors despite --kv-cache-dtype nvfp4.
next_step: Fix the vLLM/Python/JIT binding path that decides FP4 KV specialization and scale tensors for Gemma 3 paged prefill before rerunning the quality gate.

why_now: Gemma NVFP4-KV must not be blessed from routing/capacity evidence alone; the current Gemma 3 row proves heuristic quality checks can miss severe first-token corruption.
blocked_by: Gemma 3 FlashInfer paged-prefill corruption still red
acceptance: Run matched Gemma fp8-or-bf16 KV and NVFP4-KV manifests after the prefill fix.
Pass scripts/gemma_nvfp4_kv_quality_gate.py with either first-token/logprob or supplied-token PPL evidence, preferably both.
Keep the current red artifact as a regression check and do not climb to Gemma 4 31B until this gate is green.

why_now: The packaged Ubuntu22/arm64/torch2.11 SGLang image has a scoped E4B multimodal-prefix full-NVFP4 green row, but the claim-grade AR ladder is blocked by the shared 12B +0.40 long-context NVFP4 red and the E4B fp8 D512/VO256 dispatcher red.
blocked_by: Shared FlashInfer/numerics fix for the 12B long-context full-NVFP4 +0.40 red from mail 0138.
FlashInfer D512/VO256 1-byte-KV paged-prefill dispatcher fix for the E4B fp8 comparator.
acceptance: After a relevant FlashInfer/SGLang dependency changes, run the packet with the baked SGLang source-stack image or a newly documented packaged successor, not loose wheel injection.
Rerun the matched 12B bf16/fp8/full-NVFP4 shape with ctx 8185 and prefix 4096 under sequential-server GB10 memory guardrails.
Clear the E4B fp8 comparator only after the dispatcher fix, preserving the old red as the before artifact.
Run the remaining 26B-A4B and 31B matched ladder rows with the same corpus, prompt shape, backend, and image provenance.
Update RESULTS_LEDGER, WHEEL_CONTAINER_MATRIX, mail, and results summaries with exact image digest, commits, capacity ratios, quality deltas, and any residual caveats.
finding: Current SGLang packaged-image state is not broad claim-grade: E4B multimodal-prefix full-NVFP4 is scoped green, while the matched AR ladder is blocked by shared FlashInfer/numerics and fp8 dispatcher issues.

why_now: The clean full-attention Qwen path already has an accepted 8k PPL row; 32k/128k quality cost is the missing number behind the 1.7x KV capacity claim.
blocked_by: Need better watchdog/prewarm handling after the first 32k request outlived SSH access
acceptance: Run matched fp8 and NVFP4 prompt-logprob PPL at 32k and 128k with prefix caching off.
Record KV pool, PPL, delta PPL, delta nats/token, model/container commits, and explicit clean-path scope.
Exclude Gemma/SWA and SGLang radix reuse paths from this quality claim.

why_now: llama.cpp arch-build proof shows sm_121a native FP4 PTX exists, but runtime correctness and PP/TG speed versus BF16/Q8/Q4 remain separate and unproven.
acceptance: Run an actual NVFP4/MXFP4 GGUF on jethac/llama.cpp@19bba67c1 or newer pinned fork.
Capture runtime dispatch proof, correctness versus BF16/Q8 reference, and PP/TG speed versus Q4_K_M/Q8/BF16.
Do not cite BLACKWELL_NATIVE_FP4=1 alone as evidence.

why_now: The b12x gate fix proves dispatch, not tile viability. TensorRT-LLM #11368 shows SM12x FP4 GEMM can fail below dispatch when tile shared-memory use exceeds the CC-12.x per-block ceiling.
acceptance: Run the v2 microbench on a source-built FlashInfer stack with auto, b12x, cutlass, and cudnn.
Record device shared-memory limits, SM count, heuristic order, per-backend ok/error rows, finite output, cosine, latency, and approximate TFLOP/s.
Do not convert the result into a serving speed claim without model-path evidence.

why_now: The jethac/llama.cpp endpoint branch is implemented and CPU-compile checked; row #8 now needs a live GB10 smoke proving /loglikelihood scores every supplied continuation token, including the unlikely zebra case.
acceptance: Build jethac/llama.cpp@spark/hijinks-008-supplied-loglikelihood commit aa6a5961977139f23ae54dc8279fdac3d1494a77 on the Linux GB10 host.
Serve a small known GGUF model through the forked llama-server.
Run tasks/llamacpp_loglikelihood_smoke.jsonl through POST /loglikelihood or /v1/loglikelihood.
Pass scripts/llamacpp_loglikelihood_contract_audit.py before treating the path as a row-8 accuracy fix.
Preserve server log, build target evidence, contract artifact, and contract audit.

why_now: The current llama.cpp Qwen row is Qwen2.5 1.5B; the campaign needs a larger Qwen3/Qwen3.6-class GGUF practical-serving row to compare against vLLM/SGLang Qwen results.
acceptance: Serve a Qwen3/Qwen3.6-class GGUF through llama-server.
Record row manifest, OpenAI smoke, llama-bench output, build-target/runtime evidence, and model details.
Keep practical serving speed separate from paper-comparable loglikelihood accuracy.

why_now: Qwen speed/capacity is a required cross-runtime lane; using one manifest shape prevents vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp rows from becoming incomparable.
blocked_by: Requires already-running target servers or explicit server setup per row
acceptance: Run scripts/qwen_speed_lane.py with the sample input adjusted to the live servers.
Record row manifests for vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp where available.
Keep Qwen claims separate from Gemma claims unless both families have matching evidence.
